articleBody.raw: {"nodeType":"document","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"We are pleased to announce that ReportPortal has successfully completed its annual SOC 2 Type II audit for the latest reporting period. The independent assessment confirmed that our security and operational controls were appropriately designed and operated effectively throughout the audit timeframe. For enterprise security, compliance, procurement, and vendor risk teams, this renewal provides validated assurance and helps streamline vendor evaluation processes.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This marks the third consecutive year that ReportPortal has renewed its SOC 2 attestation – demonstrating that our security program is not a one-time initiative, but a sustained and mature operational discipline embedded across our organization.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"What SOC 2 Type II means","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 Type II is an independent third-party audit conducted in accordance with the AICPA Trust Services Criteria: Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Unlike a point-in-time certification, SOC 2 Type II evaluates whether controls operate effectively over a defined period. Auditors examine detailed evidence – including policies, access controls, monitoring activities, change management procedures, and risk management practices – to verify consistent execution.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"The audit requires the provision of extensive documentation and artifacts. Thanks to well-established security processes within ReportPortal and the broader governance framework of EPAM, we did not need to implement urgent remediation efforts to meet audit expectations. Instead, we demonstrated through evidence that our controls were already functioning as required.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Why this matters now","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Over the past decades, business operations have increasingly shifted online. Organizations now rely heavily on cloud-based infrastructure – including IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS models – to reduce capital expenditures and accelerate innovation. While this model increases flexibility and efficiency, it also raises expectations around data protection and operational resilience.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"In regulated industries such as financial services, non-compliance can lead to significant regulatory and reputational consequences. Standards like PCI DSS underscore the importance of maintaining strong safeguards for sensitive information.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"As businesses increasingly entrust third-party providers with confidential data, the stakes continue to rise. Organizations must ensure that sensitive data is protected from unauthorized access, misuse, or unintended exposure – including scenarios where private data could be improperly used, for example, to train large language models (LLMs).","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"At the same time, vendor security questionnaires alone are often insufficient. Security teams cannot always independently verify vendor statements. SOC 2 addresses this challenge by providing independent validation based on auditor-reviewed evidence – not just written assurances.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Today, SOC 2 has effectively become a baseline requirement for enterprise third-party risk management programs. Security maturity must extend across the entire supply chain. It is not enough for an organization to be secure internally – its vendors must meet comparable standards. Independent audits help ensure there are no weak links, because once a security incident occurs, remediation is already reactive.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Our multi-year SOC 2 renewal demonstrates long-term commitment to maintaining these expectations.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"What this means for ReportPortal customers","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This renewal directly supports customer needs by enabling faster vendor onboarding, easier security reviews, and increased confidence in ReportPortal’s security program.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"For enterprise procurement and security teams, a current SOC 2 Type II attestation reduces evaluation friction by providing independently validated assurance of control effectiveness. It simplifies due diligence processes and supports internal approval workflows.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"By maintaining SOC 2 compliance year after year, we help our customers move forward with greater confidence in the strength and stability of our security controls.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Security as an ongoing commitment","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Beyond supporting customer reviews and onboarding processes, SOC 2 renewal reflects a broader, long-term security commitment. SOC 2 renewal is part of a broader, continuous security strategy. Security at ReportPortal is integrated across organizational processes and supported at every level. We maintain structured risk assessments, ongoing monitoring, governance controls, and continuous improvement practices to ensure our safeguards remain effective as our platform evolves.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"By renewing SOC 2 for the third consecutive year, ReportPortal reinforces its commitment to transparency, accountability, and enterprise-grade security standards.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 vs. SOC 3: understanding the difference","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"To support different stages of vendor evaluation, ReportPortal provides both SOC 2 and SOC 3 reports:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 Type II report","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"\nA detailed report that describes our control environment, testing procedures, and auditor results. Because it contains sensitive information about internal controls, it is typically shared under NDA upon request. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 3 report\n","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"A high-level summary confirming successful completion of the SOC 2 audit. It does not include detailed control descriptions and can be shared publicly without NDA.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"For many organizations, the SOC 3 report provides sufficient initial assurance to proceed with evaluation, while the SOC 2 Type II report offers deeper transparency for full security reviews. When customers need assurance for periods after the SOC report end date, we can also provide ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"bridge letters","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" – management attestations summarizing any material changes to our control environment since the last report, helping close gaps between audit cycles during vendor risk reviews.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"To request the ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 Type II report or SOC 3 report","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":", please reach out via our official ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/contact-us/general"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"contact form","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":".","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Our team is ready to support your security and compliance evaluation process.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

title.title: ReportPortal renews SOC 2 Type II attestation for the third consecutive year

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: We are pleased to announce that ReportPortal has successfully completed its annual SOC 2 Type II audit for the latest reporting period.

articleBody.raw: {"nodeType":"document","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal is widely used for analyzing automated test results, aggregating logs, classifying failures with ML, and tracking quality trends over time. When used correctly, it significantly improves feedback speed and decision-making.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"However, many teams fail to unlock its full potential. The problem is rarely the tool itself – it’s how teams analyze and interpret the data.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Below are the most common mistakes teams make when working with test results in ReportPortal, along with practical advice on how to avoid them.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"1. Analyzing reports without a clear goal","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"A frequent mistake is reviewing reports without a specific purpose. Teams open a dashboard, notice the number of failed tests, and move on.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal provides data, not conclusions. Without clear questions, analysis remains superficial. Unhelpful questions:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How many tests failed?\"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"\"Is the build green?\"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":" Better questions:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Which failure types are increasing over time?","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Which tests are the flakiest?","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Where do we lose the most time during triage?","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:\n","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"Define the goal before analyzing results – release quality, test stability, feedback speed, or technical debt. Build ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/dashboards-and-widgets/PossibleDashboardsInReportPortal"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"dashboards","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" around that goal.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"2. Ignoring custom defect types","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Many teams rely only on default ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/work-with-reports/InvestigationOfFailure#set-defect-type"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"defect types","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" (Product Bug, Automation Bug, System Issue, No Defect, To Investigate) and never go further. As a result:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"all failures look the same","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ML classification becomes less effective","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal delivers real value only when defect types are used consistently.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Common issue:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"\nEverything is marked as \"To Investigate\", and nothing gets properly categorized.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Define a clear set of defect types and keep them up to date with your needs","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Analyze each failure and explicitly assign the correct defect type – this creates training data for the ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/analysis/AutoAnalysisOfLaunches"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Analyzer","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":", so similar issues can be automatically recognized and categorized correctly in future runs","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"3. Blind trust in ML classification","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal’s ML features are powerful, but they are not a replacement for process. Some teams either ignore ML entirely or trust it without verification.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Problems occur when:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"training data is inconsistent","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"predictions are not reviewed","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"test logs are unstable","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Regularly confirm or correct ML predictions. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Ensure consistent logging for similar failures. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Treat ML as an assistant, not a decision-maker","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"4. Mixing incompatible test runs in one analysis","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Another common mistake is analyzing tests with different purposes together:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"UI and API tests ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"smoke and regression suites","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This makes metrics misleading. A failure rate acceptable for nightly runs may be unacceptable for smoke tests.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Separate launches by test purpose. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Use ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/work-with-reports/LaunchesTestItemsAttributes"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"attributes","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" or separate projects.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"5. Underestimating the importance of log quality","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal cannot compensate for poor logging. If logs are inconsistent or lack context, analysis becomes manual and slow. Typical issues:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"the same error reported differently","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"missing steps or environment details","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"no clear failure reason","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Standardize test logging.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Log causes, not just symptoms.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Ensure similar failures produce similar messages.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"6. No clear ownership of test result analysis","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"A critical organizational mistake is having \"shared ownership\" of ReportPortal – meaning no ownership at all. When no one is responsible:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"dashboards degrade over time","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"defect classification becomes inconsistent","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"insights are lost","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Assign a clear owner (for example, QA Lead).","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Continuously improve dashboards and metrics.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Treat ReportPortal as a product, not just a log repository.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal is a decision-support tool, not just a reporting system. Most mistakes teams make are process-related, not technical. Teams that define clear analysis goals, invest in defect classification and logging quality, and assign ownership, turn ReportPortal into a powerful driver of quality and efficiency rather than a source of noisy data.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

title.title: Common mistakes when analyzing test results with ReportPortal

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: ReportPortal is widely used for analyzing automated test results, aggregating logs, classifying failures with ML, and tracking quality trends over time. When used correctly, it significantly improves feedback speed and decision-making.

articleBody.raw: {"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Testing in the insurance sector is inherently complex. Any change – whether a new insurance product, updated pricing rules, regulatory adjustment, or claims workflow enhancement – impacts multiple interconnected systems. These may include policy administration, underwriting engines, claims management platforms, billing systems, customer portals, mobile applications, and third-party integrations.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Ensuring that all these components work together correctly requires extensive test coverage, reliable evidence, and clear visibility across teams and environments. ReportPortal helps insurance organizations manage this complexity by centralizing test results, automating analysis, and providing actionable insights for confident release decisions.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"blueBg Explore our ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/pricing/saas"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"value":"pricing plans","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" to see how easily you can get started. ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Below, we explore how ReportPortal supports insurance companies in delivering stable, compliant, and high-quality digital solutions.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"1. Unified View of Insurance Product Testing","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Launching or modifying an insurance product affects multiple systems at once. For example, a new policy offering may require validation of premium calculations, underwriting rules, document generation, billing schedules, and customer-facing portals.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"These test scenarios are often owned by different teams and executed using different tools. ReportPortal brings all product-related test results into a ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/features/UnifiedTestReporting"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"value":"single, unified view","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":", allowing teams to assess readiness based on consistent ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/dashboards-and-widgets/ReportingAndMetricsInReportPortal#qa-and-test-automation-metrics-in-reportportal"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"value":"metrics","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" and shared quality indicators.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"2. Consolidated Cross-System Validation Evidence","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Insurance workflows typically span API services, UI flows, batch processes, and external integrations such as payment providers or regulatory systems. ReportPortal aggregates results from all these layers and becomes a single source of truth for end-to-end validation.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Regardless of the ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/log-data-in-reportportal/test-framework-integration/"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"value":"test framework","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" or test type used, stakeholders can review consolidated evidence and confirm that every critical system involved in the insurance process behaves as expected.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"3. Full Control Over Sensitive Insurance Test Data","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Insurance organizations deal with personal, financial, and sometimes medical information. ReportPortal supports on-premises and private cloud deployments, allowing organizations to keep full control over their test data, logs, and attachments.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"ReportPortal is designed to support compliance with industry and regulatory requirements. Our test automation reporting dashboard undergoes regular independent audits and certifications, including SOC 2 and ISO standards, which helps insurance companies align with GDPR, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and regional data protection regulations.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"4. Audit-Ready Reporting for Regulatory Requirements","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Regulatory compliance is a constant requirement in the insurance industry. During audits, teams must provide clear proof that systems were properly tested and validated.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"ReportPortal addresses this need through ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/configuration/LaunchesRetentionPolicy"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"value":"important launches","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":", which allows teams to mark critical test runs and exclude them from standard retention policies, ensuring long-term preservation of key testing data. In addition, you can ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/work-with-reports/OperationsUnderLaunches#export-launches-reports"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"value":"export selected launches","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" together with all logs and attachments, creating a portable and secure archive of testing evidence.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Together, these capabilities help retain audit-ready documentation, significantly reducing preparation time and audit-related risks.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"5. Efficient Testing of Complex Insurance Workflows","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Insurance processes such as claims handling, renewals, endorsements, and fraud detection involve complex business rules and multiple decision points. Validating these workflows requires running large regression suites with thousands of scenarios.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"ReportPortal is designed to handle high test volumes and intensive execution rates, simplifying result analysis even for the most complex insurance workflows.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"6. Faster Failure Analysis With AI-Assisted Insights","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Large insurance regression suites often produce repetitive or flaky failures. ReportPortal applies ","nodeType":"text"},{"data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/analysis/AutoAnalysisOfLaunches"},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"value":"AI and machine learning","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"hyperlink"},{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":" to automatically group similar issues, detect patterns, and reduce noise in test results.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"This enables QA teams to focus on real product defects, shorten triage time, and improve overall testing efficiency.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"7. Environment-Aware Defect Classification","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Insurance systems frequently operate across multiple environments – legacy platforms, modern microservices, test sandboxes, and third-party services. title.title: Benefits of ReportPortal for the insurance sector

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: Testing in the insurance sector is inherently complex. Any change – whether a new insurance product, updated pricing rules, regulatory adjustment, or claims workflow enhancement – impacts multiple interconnected systems.
